title: Count Provinces from Friendship Matrix
description:
You are given an n×n 0/1 matrix isConnected where isConnected[i][j]=1 means cities i and j have a direct connection. A province is a group of directly or indirectly connected cities. Return the number of provinces. Cities are 0-indexed in the input for coding convenience.

    

Constraints:
    - 1 ≤ n ≤ 200
- Matrix is symmetric with ones on the diagonal
explanation:
Intuition
Treat the matrix as an undirected graph adjacency matrix. Counting connected components via DFS or BFS yields the number of provinces.

Approach
1. Initial Setup: Allocate a visited set
2. Main Algorithm: For each unvisited node, run DFS/BFS to mark its component
3. Return Result: Count how many times we start a new traversal

Complexity Analysis
- Time Complexity: O(n^2) to scan neighbors
- Space Complexity: O(n) for visited

Key Insights
- Matrix neighbors are row indices with value 1
- Either DFS or BFS works equally here

Common Pitfalls
- Forgetting to mark nodes visited causes repeated work
- Assuming the matrix is not symmetric will miscount

testCases.explanation: Cities 0 and 1 form one province; 2 is alone

hints: Think in terms of connected components
Use a visited set to avoid repeats
DFS or BFS both work on the matrix rows
